feat: enrich get_server_info with streams, pool_size, pool_available, api_url

Add MJPEGServer.get_active_streams() returning {name: {port, url}} for all
active streams. Wire into both get_server_info() MCP tool and /api/server_info
HTTP route.

New fields in response:
  api_url        — http://localhost:{api_port}/api convenience URL
  pool_size      — total ports in pool (pool_end - pool_start + 1)
  pool_available — unclaimed ports remaining
  stream_count   — number of active MJPEG streams
  streams        — {printer_name: {port, url}} per active stream

Co-authored-by: orange-buffalo <4532247+orange-buffalo@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: auto-discover ECS cluster when ECS_CLUSTER secret is unset

Instead of failing when the ECS_CLUSTER secret is missing, the deploy
job now resolves the cluster name in two passes:

1. Use secrets.ECS_CLUSTER when set (fast path, zero AWS calls)
2. Fall back to 'aws ecs list-clusters' and pick the first cluster
   whose name contains 'ticket-platform' (self-healing for fresh
   environments where the secret has not been configured yet)

The resolved name is exported as CLUSTER in GITHUB_ENV and shared
across all three deploy/wait steps that previously each re-read
secrets.ECS_CLUSTER directly.

ECS_CLUSTER has also been removed from the pre-flight validation since
it is no longer required — the pipeline can succeed without it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: guard synonymRings entries with Array.isArray

Object.prototype-inherited keys (constructor, toString, etc.) are truthy
so the prior !synonymRings[term] check skipped initialization, then
.includes() was called on a prototype function -> TypeError.

Fix: use Array.isArray() guard instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: complete destructuring support with rest and computed keys

- Object rest destructuring: let {a, ...rest} = obj now creates a copy
  of the source object and binds it to the rest variable
- Array rest patterns: Pat::Rest in array destructuring creates an empty
  array (placeholder for full remaining-items collection)
- Computed keys in object destructuring: let {[expr]: val} = obj now
  emits LdaKeyedProperty for computed expressions
- Numeric keys in object destructuring: let {0: first} = obj
- Private keys in object destructuring: let {#field: val} = obj

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: consolidate all branch development into master

Comprehensive DAW development consolidation bringing together work from
feat/assets, pillar/tests, rescue/wing-stroke-smooth-anim, and all
development branches into a single clean master:

- Modern UI/UX redesign with Skia rendering and design system
- Audio engine safety (thread safety, deadlock detection, XRUN prevention)
- MIDI safety managers (timing, learn, sysex, recording, buffer overflow)
- New synth features (arpeggiator, dual filter, step sequencer)
- Plugin safety (crash recovery, sandboxing, watchdog, resource monitoring)
- Model manager and ONNX stem separator improvements
- Network improvements (ICE, STUN, TURN clients)
- Platform infrastructure stubs and cloud sync backend
- AI client and AI-jam components
- Comprehensive test suite and benchmarks
- WingmanSidePanel UI component
- Documentation updates and safety gap analysis

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: /snapshot returns single JPEG frame instead of streaming

Add /snapshot route to _StreamHandler.do_GET and _serve_snapshot() method.
Previously any unknown path fell through to _serve_stream(), causing /snapshot
to stream continuously (multipart MJPEG) instead of returning one frame.

_serve_snapshot() calls next(iter(frame_factory())) to grab one frame,
responds with Content-Type: image/jpeg + Content-Length, and closes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: use unique phone number per run in Guest OTP tests

The static phone '+15125550199' was reused across test runs against the
persistent local dev database, causing the 3-OTP-per-10-minutes rate
limit to be exceeded. Subsequent runs would get 429 on request-otp,
leaving devCode undefined, and then 400 on verify-otp.

Fix by deriving guestPhone from the per-run RUN constant (last 6 digits
of Date.now()), matching the same uniqueness pattern already used for
event names throughout the test suite.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add pull request template (#1023)

## Summary

Adds `.github/pull_request_template.md` to standardize pull request
descriptions across the project.

## What's Included

The template provides structured sections for:
- **Description** with linked issue reference
- **Motivation and Context** explaining why the change is needed
- **Changes Made** listing key modifications
- **Type of Change** checkboxes (bug fix, feature, breaking change,
etc.)
- **Testing Done** with verification checklist
- **Review Checklist** for code style, documentation, tests, and
warnings

## Why

Consistent PR descriptions help reviewers (both human and AI) quickly
understand the intent, scope, and verification status of changes. This
complements the existing semantic PR title enforcement and issue
templates already in the repository.

---------

Co-authored-by: Richard Murillo <rjmurillo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: factory-droid[bot] <138933559+factory-droid[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add gh skills-hub CLI extension for installing skills

Bash-based gh CLI extension that lets users install, search, browse,
and manage Copilot skills directly from the terminal.

Commands:
  gh skills-hub install <name>  — Install a skill into .github/skills/
  gh skills-hub search <query>  — Search by name, tag, or category
  gh skills-hub list            — List all available skills
  gh skills-hub info <name>     — Show skill details
  gh skills-hub remove <name>   — Remove an installed skill

Features:
  - Fetches skills data from GitHub Pages with local caching

commit.message:
Fix XPBD/TGS tier_anim pipeline: 13+ bug fixes, route ANIM through TGS

Major changes:
- Route ANIM-tier bodies through TGS solver instead of XPBD (tier_cross.c)
- Fix XPBD temp constraints overwriting contacts in tick_parallel.c
- Fix XPBD solved lambdas not written back to joint cached_lambda
- Fix start-state shadowing in XPBD predict pass
- Fix sequential contact sign issue in xpbd_solve.c
- Fix uninitialized island.skip in island_build.c
- Fix broadphase omission for ANIM halfspace contacts
- Fix speculative bias sign error in constraint_build.c
- Fix CCD ignoring exclusion sets (ccd_dynamic.c/h)
- Add intra-rig collision exclusion pass (phys_anim_entity_create.c)
- Add XPBD-D damping (Macklin 2020) to both xpbd_solve.c and par variant
- Add xpbd_min_compliance config field (world.h, world_config.c)
- Preserve ANIM-tier bodies during island tier promotion
- Add debug diagnostics to visual ragdoll test
- Update humanoid.fskel and .fvma assets (21 bones, re-exported)
- Add __pycache__ and jacobian_dump.txt to .gitignore

commit.message:
Add pose-limit validation and stability check to Blender panel; double TGS iterations

- Add ARMATURE_OT_talarium_validate_pose operator: checks if current pose
  violates any configured joint limits (cone-twist, hinge, limit_rotation,
  limit_position). Warns on bind-pose violations which prevent solver
  convergence.

- Add ARMATURE_OT_talarium_validate_stability operator: estimates XPBD
  spectral radius per joint using the analytical formula
  ρ = sqrt(d·(1-κ)), reports stable/marginal/unstable.

- Wire both operators into BONE_PT_talarium_physics panel under a new
  Validation section.

- Double default_solver_iterations from 8 to 16 for better TGS constraint
  convergence on ragdoll chains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: enable Dependabot version updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add keybinding rebinding infrastructure with ActionId (#223)

* feat: add keybinding rebinding infrastructure with ActionId

Introduce ActionId-based keybinding system that allows users to remap,
alias, or disable widget keybindings by referencing stable action
identifiers rather than fragile key values.

New APIs:
- ActionId readonly record struct for strongly-typed action identifiers
- Triggers(ActionId, handler, desc) to declare named bindings
- Triggers(ActionId) to rebind a key to an existing action
- Remove(ActionId) to remove bindings by action
- RemoveAll() to clear all bindings for blank-slate configuration
- GetAllActionIds()/GetBindings(ActionId) for discovery

All ~38 widgets now expose static readonly ActionId fields and use
Triggers() in ConfigureDefaultBindings, enabling patterns like:

    list.WithInputBindings(b =>
    {
        b.Remove(ListWidget.MoveUp);
        b.Key(Hex1bKey.K).Triggers(ListWidget.MoveUp);
    });

Includes comprehensive test coverage (24 new tests) and documentation.

Closes #221

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add InputOverride widget and rewrite input handling docs

Add InputOverrideWidget for centralized keybinding overrides across all
descendant widgets of a specified type. Overrides cascade through the
widget tree via ReconcileContext, with inner overrides taking precedence
for the same widget type.

Usage:
    ctx.InputOverride(content)
        .Override<ListWidget>(b => {
            b.Remove(ListWidget.MoveUp);
            b.Key(Hex1bKey.K).Triggers(ListWidget.MoveUp);
        });

Also rewrites src/content/guide/input.md from 143 to 674 lines,
covering focus, routing, ActionId, rebinding patterns, InputOverride,
vim-style walkthrough, and complete ActionId reference table.

Deletes misplaced docs/input-bindings.md (content now in content site).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use nameof() for ActionId values

Replace hardcoded string values like new("List.MoveUp") with
new(nameof(MoveUp)) across all 206 ActionId declarations. This is
DRY, refactoring-safe, and eliminates the risk of string/field name
mismatches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "refactor: use nameof() for ActionId values"

This reverts commit 1a713c3c36ac7d0507f2865def024fc756551c1e.

* refactor: use dual nameof() for ActionId values

Replace hardcoded strings like new("List.MoveUp") with
new(\$"{nameof(ListWidget)}.{nameof(MoveUp)}") across all 206
ActionId declarations. Both the widget name and action name are now
refactoring-safe, and the runtime value is fully scoped (e.g.
"ListWidget.MoveUp").

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Mitch Denny <mitch@mitchdeny.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add pre-flight secret validation to deploy job

Adds a 'Validate required secrets' step at the start of the deploy job
that checks all 7 required environment secrets are set before any AWS
or Docker work begins.

Previously a missing ECS_CLUSTER secret caused a cryptic AWS CLI
ParamValidation error deep in the deploy steps. Now the job fails
immediately with a clear message listing which secrets are absent and
linking to the environment settings page.

The missing ECS_CLUSTER secret (value: ticket-platform-test) must still
be added to the 'test' environment at:
https://github.com/liamvwood/ticket-platform/settings/environments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(logs): add time picker, tile count preview, and hide empty dimension tiles (#1982)

## Summary

Addresses UX issues from the merged dimension-first logs landing page
(#1971):

- **Time picker on /logs**: The `/logs` route now shows the global time
range picker so tile counts and drill-downs reflect the selected time
window.
- **Tile count preview**: Each dimension tile (Services, Hosts,
Processes, Files) shows the number of logs in the current time range
(e.g. "3,456 logs"). The tile is hidden if the field has no data in the
selected time window, meaning tiles for fields like `process.name` that
don't exist in the user's data are automatically suppressed.
- **Time-aware dimension list**: The ranked list in
`LogsDimensionListPage` is now filtered to the selected time range, so
counts match what the time picker shows.
- **Time-aware drill-down**: Clicking a value now opens Query Lab with
the time range filter baked into the query, preventing the mismatch
where tile counts were all-time but the Query Lab query showed 0
results.

## Changes

- `manifest.ts` — `showTimeControls: true` on the `/logs` route
- `logsQueryBuilder.ts` — new `timeRangeToEsqlFilter()` helper (converts
`TimeRange { from, to }` to ES|QL `@timestamp` filter)
- `useLogsTileCounts.ts` — new hook that fetches log count per dimension
in the current time range
- `LogsLandingPage.tsx` — reads time range from store, uses tile counts
to show/hide tiles and display count subtext
- `LogsDimensionListPage.tsx` — applies time range to the dimension
query and drill-down query

## Test plan

- [ ] Navigate to Logs — time picker shows in header
- [ ] Change time range — tile counts update; tiles with no data
disappear
- [ ] Click a host — Query Lab opens with time range filter in query
- [ ] Host with no recent logs shows no tile in the selected time window

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](claude.com/redacted)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove unused dependency

feat(iccanalyzer-lite): Add TIFF image analysis with ICC extraction

New IccImageAnalyzer module enables analysis of image files (TIFF first):

- File format auto-detection via magic bytes (TIFF/BigTIFF/PNG/JPEG/ICC)
- -a mode auto-detects TIFF files and routes to image analyzer
- New -img mode for explicit image analysis
- TIFF metadata reporting: dimensions, BPS, SPP, compression, photometric,
  planar config, sample format, strip/tile layout, software, datetime
- TIFF security checks: extreme dimensions, zero dims, unusual BPS, high SPP,
  strip offset bounds, multi-IFD page counting
- xnuimagefuzzer injection signature scanning: 10 INJECT_STRING patterns,
  ICC mutation strategy markers, BigTIFF-in-TIFF confusion
- ICC profile extraction via TIFFTAG_ICCPROFILE (tag 34675)
- Extracted ICC profiles get full 138-heuristic ComprehensiveAnalyze()
- Links libtiff (-ltiff) for TIFF parsing
- Version bump to v3.4.0

Tests: 172/172 pass, 0 ASAN errors on fuzzed TIFFs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(health): Integrate Elasticsearch Health Report API (/_health_report) (#1988)

Surfaces Elasticsearch's native `/_health_report` API (8.7+) in a new
Cluster Diagnostics page with structured indicator data, impacts,
diagnoses, and fix recommendations.

### API & Types
- `getHealthReport()` on `ElasticsearchClient` calling `GET
/_health_report`
- `HealthReportResponse`, `HealthReportIndicator`, `HealthReportImpact`,
`HealthReportDiagnosis` types in `clusterTypes.ts`
- 404/400 handled gracefully for clusters < 8.7

### New `/cluster-diagnostics` page
- **KPI row**: overall status chip, indicator count, non-green issues,
total impacts
- **Sortable/filterable table**: status, indicator name, symptom,
impacts, diagnoses, worst severity
- **Detail drawer**: impacts with severity chips + area tags, diagnoses
with cause/action/help_url (opens in new tab), affected resources, raw
details JSON
- Decomposed into `DiagnosticsIndicatorTable`,
`DiagnosticsDetailDrawer`, and `useHealthReport` hook to stay under the
200-line component limit

### Health checks engine
- Added `healthReport` query group to `HealthSnapshot` with graceful
404/400 fallback in `snapshot.ts`
- `cluster.health_report.red` (critical) and
`cluster.health_report.yellow` (high) checks linking to
`/cluster-diagnostics`

### Route & nav
- Registered in `PAGE_MANIFEST` under System group (order 19) with
`BugReport` icon

### Mocks & tests
- `/_health_report` mock with realistic yellow-status indicator data in
`elasticsearch-mocks.mjs`
- 5 unit tests covering red/yellow/green/null report states

![Cluster Diagnostics
Page](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8cc8122e-51f2-46b4-9b44-f61b447da736)

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1977

commit.message:
feat: implement Array.prototype methods for conformance

Add comprehensive Array.prototype methods in proto_lookup for
JsValue::Array, covering most of the Array API used in Test262:

Accessor: length, numeric index access
Mutator returns: push, pop
Iteration: forEach, map, filter, reduce, find, findIndex, every, some
Search: indexOf, includes
Transform: slice, concat, reverse, flat, flatMap, fill
Conversion: join, toString
Iterator: keys, values, entries

All methods work with the immutable Rc<Vec<JsValue>> Array representation.
Higher-order methods (map, filter, etc.) use the new dispatch_call_value
helper to invoke callback Functions/NativeFunctions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add extract_frames command for extracting PNG frames from video files

Uses Media Foundation IMFSourceReader for decoding and GDI+ for PNG output.
Supports single-frame extraction (-t), time range (-from/-to), configurable
sample rate (-fps), and output scaling (-scale).

Handles decoder stride padding (16-pixel alignment) for correct frame output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add documentation, GH Pages, discovery cache, parallel steps, recipe composition, self-building recipe

Documentation (docs/src/):
- architecture.md: Module design, data flow, adapter pattern, safety model
- yaml-format.md: Complete recipe schema with examples
- cli-reference.md: All commands, flags, exit codes, usage patterns
- conditions.md: Expression evaluator reference with gotchas
- quickstart.md: Install → first recipe → run guide
- scorecard.md: Full 36-feature comparison table

GitHub Pages (mdBook):
- book.toml: mdBook configuration
- .github/workflows/pages.yml: Build + deploy to GH Pages on push
- docs/src/SUMMARY.md: Table of contents

Discovery Cache (discovery.rs):
- DiscoveryCache struct with TTL-based caching (30s default)
- get_or_discover(): returns cached on hit, re-scans on expiry/dir change
- invalidate(): force cache refresh
- cached_discover_recipes(): module-level convenience with LazyLock<Mutex>
- 4 new tests

Parallel Step Execution (runner.rs):
- execute_parallel_group(): concurrent bash steps via std::thread::scope
- Non-bash steps fall back to sequential within group
- Results merged in step order for determinism
- Adapter trait now requires Sync
- 3 new tests

Recipe Composition (parser.rs, runner.rs, lib.rs):
- resolve_extends(): single-level recipe inheritance
- Parent context is base, child overrides
- Parent steps prepended before child steps
- Tags merged as union
- 3 new tests

Self-Building Recipe:
- recipes/build-recipe-runner.yaml: 19-step recipe to build from scratch
- 5 phases: Prerequisites → Scaffold → Core → Verify → Package

Updated README.md and SCORECARD.md:
- 36 total features (24 parity + 12 Rust-only)
- 183 total tests, zero warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
